perturbation.original_text: 3.  CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS 3.1. In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS 3.1. In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use. 3.2. Notwithstanding the above, the Receiving party is free to disclose any information, as long as the disclousure do not include the NSK Europe Limited trademark
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a clause (3.2) allowing the Receiving Party to disclose information as long as it doesn't include the NSK Europe Limited trademark. This directly contradicts clause 3.1(c), which states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information in whole or in part. This creates an ambiguity: is the Receiving Party allowed to disclose Confidential Information (as per 3.2), or are they forbidden from doing so (as per 3.1(c))?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. In consideration of the Disclosing Party disclosing Confidential Information to the Receiving Party, the Receiv ing Party undertakes that it shall: a) keep the Confidential Inf ormation secret and confidential, using at least the same degree of care as its uses to protect its own confidential information but no less than a reasonable degree of care ; b) not use or exploit t he Confidential Information in any way except for the Purpose; c) not disclose or make available any Confidential Information in whole or in part to any person except as expressly permitted by and in accordance with this Agreement ; and d) to establish and maint ain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorized access or use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces clause 3.2, which allows the Receiving Party to disclose information as long as it doesn't include the NSK Europe Limited trademark. This directly contradicts clause 3.1(c) of the original text and the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information in whole or in part. This creates a direct and clear in-text contradiction, fulfilling the criteria for a "YES" assignment based on Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 5.1. Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files . 5.2. Clause 5.1 of this Agreement shall not apply to: a) Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back -up systems which are not otherwise reasonably retrievable by the Representatives of the Receiving Party or its Affiliates; or b) Copies of Confidential Information which must be retained by the Receiving Party pursuant to applicable law .
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RETURN OR DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 5.1. Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files . 5.2. Clause 5.1 of this Agreement shall not apply to: a) Confidential Information held electronically in archive or back -up systems which are not otherwise reasonably retrievable by the Representatives of the Receiving Party or its Affiliates; or b) Copies of Confidential Information which must be retained by the Receiving Party pursuant to applicable law . 5.3. Notwithstanding 5.1, the receiving party can use the Disclosing party Confidential information as needed until this agreement is not effective anymore
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 5.3 states that the receiving party can use the Disclosing party Confidential information as needed until this agreement is not effective anymore. This contradicts clause 5.1 which states that upon request, the receiving party must return or destroy all confidential information. This creates uncertainty about what to do with the information at the end of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Upon the Disclosing Party ’s written request , the Receiving Party shall (as requested by the Disclosing Party) either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be confirmed in writing by the Receiving Party) all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party including all copies, reproductions, notes , extracts and summaries which include, reflect, incorporate or otherwise contain the Disclosing Party’s Confidenti al Infor mation whether in tangible form or otherwise, such as electronic mail or computer files .
perturbation.justification: The added clause 5.3 in the changed text states that the receiving party can use the disclosing party's confidential information as needed until the agreement is no longer effective. This directly contradicts clause 5.1 (which is also the contradicted text) which mandates that upon the disclosing party's written request, the receiving party must either return or destroy all confidential information. This creates a strong contradiction because the receiving party has conflicting obligations: to both use the information until the agreement ends and to return or destroy it upon request. This aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, as the added clause introduces conflicting implications, creating uncertainty about obligations and rights.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1. The term of this Agreement shall be fo r a period of [three (3) ] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”) but may be terminated or extended upon both Parties’ written agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1. The term of this Agreement shall be fo r a period of [three (3) ] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”). The Term may be terminated by NSK Europe Limited at any time, without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: Clause 10.1 originally states that the term of the agreement is three years and can only be terminated or extended by written agreement of both parties. The modification allows NSK Europe Limited to terminate the agreement at any time without notice. This directly contradicts the original condition that both parties must agree in writing for termination, creating uncertainty about the circumstances under which the contract can be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1. The term of this Agreement shall be fo r a period of [three (3) ] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”) but may be terminated or extended upon both Parties’ written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement term is three years and can only be terminated or extended by written agreement of both parties. The changed text allows NSK Europe Limited to terminate the agreement at any time without notice. This directly contradicts the original condition that both parties must agree in writing for termination. This modification directly violates the term that both parties must agree in writing for termination, making the terms inconsistent and creating a strong contradiction as defined in the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1. Assignment and other dealings. Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement without the prior written agreement of the other Party .
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1. Assignment and other dealings. Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement. NSK Europe Limited may assign or transfer its rights at any time without the other party's written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text of clause 12.1 states that neither party can assign or transfer their rights without the written agreement of the other party. The modification introduces an exception, granting NSK Europe Limited the right to assign or transfer its rights at any time without the other party's agreement. This directly contradicts the initial requirement that both parties must agree in writing for such actions, creating uncertainty and unequal power dynamics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1. Assignment and other dealings. Neither Party shall assign, transfer, subcontract or deal in any other manner with any of its rights and obligations under this Agreement without the prior written agreement of the other Party .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies clause 12.1 to allow NSK Europe Limited to assign or transfer its rights without the other party's written agreement. This directly contradicts the original clause 12.1 (also the contradicted text) that states neither party can assign or transfer their rights without the written agreement of the other party. The modification introduces an exception that is explicitly forbidden in the contradicted text.
